name: Muddy Waters
abstract: McKinley Morganfield (April 4, 1913 – April 30, 1983), better known as Muddy Waters, was an American blues singer-songwriter and musician who was an important figure in the post-World War II blues scene, and is often cited as the "father of modern Chicago blues". Muddy Waters grew up on Stovall Plantation near Clarksdale, Mississippi, an…

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: I just did a piece for the Wall Street Journal on a 46-year-old guy named Barrelhouse Chuck Goering... telling stories about Otis Spann and not only Otis Spann but Muddy Waters, who was one of his mentors.

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Cotton
about_quotes.evidence: I remember one time we played with Muddy Waters in Los Angeles, man.

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Muddy Waters only in response to the interviewer's question about an anecdote involving Muddy Waters and Keith Richards. Ramsey Lewis does not claim any personal interaction, professional relationship, or direct knowledge of Muddy Waters beyond what was presented in the int

about_quotes.speaker: Professor Longhair (Henry Roeland Byrd)
about_quotes.evidence: All these old blues, like Lowell Folsom and Muddy Water and Clean Head Benson, I mean those are really, they're, they're, they're not just somebody helped them up there.

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember Muddy Waters said, “The blues got pregnant and the baby was rock and roll.” [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Right, right, right, right.
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Thomas interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 2014
